actor_claims.actor_label: any person or group of persons
actor_claims.quote_text: any person or group of persons with respect to any alternative proposals

actor_claims.actor_label: any person that has made or is considering making an alternative proposal
actor_claims.quote_text: any person that has made or, to the Company’s knowledge, is considering making an alternative proposal

actor_claims.actor_label: the person who has made the alternative proposal
actor_claims.quote_text: the person who has made the alternative proposal

event_claims.actor_label: the Company and its subsidiaries, and their respective representatives
event_claims.description: During the go-shop period, the Company, its subsidiaries and their representatives were permitted to initiate, solicit or encourage alternative proposal inquiries or offers.
event_claims.quote_text: until 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013 (the “go shop period”), the Company and its subsidiaries, and their respective representatives, were permitted to:

event_claims.actor_label: the Company and its subsidiaries, and their respective representatives
event_claims.description: The Company and its subsidiaries were permitted during the go-shop period to initiate, solicit or encourage inquiries or proposals constituting alternative proposals.
event_claims.quote_text: initiate, solicit or encourage any inquiry or the making of any proposal or offer that constitutes an alternative proposal

event_claims.actor_label: the Company and its subsidiaries, and their respective representatives
event_claims.description: The Company and its subsidiaries were permitted during the go-shop period to engage in discussions or negotiations with persons or groups concerning alternative proposals.
event_claims.quote_text: engage in, enter into, continue or otherwise participate in any discussions or negotiations with any person or group of persons with respect to any alternative proposals

event_claims.description: The Company was required to notify Hudson’s Bay of each person or group from whom it received a written alternative proposal before the go-shop deadline.
event_claims.quote_text: The Company was required to promptly (and in any event within two business days) notify Hudson’s Bay of the identity of each person or group from whom the Company received a written alternative proposal after the execution of the merger agreement and prior to 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013

event_claims.description: The Company was required to provide Hudson’s Bay with a list of persons or groups that submitted written alternative proposals that could reasonably be expected to result in superior proposals, with each such person or group treated as an excluded party.
event_claims.quote_text: the Company was required to provide Hudson’s Bay with a list of persons or groups from whom the Company or its representatives received prior to 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013, a written alternative proposal that the board determined in good faith, after consultation with outside counsel and financial advisors, was or could reasonably be expected to result in, a superior proposal

event_claims.description: The Company announced on September 10, 2013 that the go-shop period had expired on September 6, 2013 and that no party had been designated as an excluded party.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 10, 2013, the Company announced that the go shop period expired on September 6, 2013, and no party had been designated by the Company as an excluded party.

event_claims.description: Since the go-shop deadline, the Company and its subsidiaries were required to cease prior solicitation activities and ongoing discussions or negotiations concerning alternative proposals, except as permitted for excluded parties or under the merger agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: since 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013, the Company and its subsidiaries have been and are required to, and the Company has been and is required to cause its and its subsidiaries’ representatives to, cease any activities described above and any discussions or negotiations with any person or group that may have been ongoing with respect to any alternative proposals.

event_claims.description: From September 6, 2013 until the effective time or termination, the Company was subject to a no-solicitation covenant prohibiting it from initiating, soliciting, encouraging or facilitating alternative proposal inquiries, proposals or offers, subject to exceptions.
event_claims.quote_text: from 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013 until the effective time or, if earlier, the termination of the merger agreement, the Company has been and is required to not, and to cause its subsidiaries and its and their respective representatives not to, directly or indirectly:

event_claims.description: Following the go-shop deadline, if the Company received a written alternative proposal before shareholder approval, it and its representatives could contact the proposer to clarify terms.
event_claims.quote_text: if following 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013 and prior to obtaining the required vote of the Company’s shareholders to approve the merger agreement, the Company receives a written alternative proposal, the Company and its representatives may contact the person who has made the alternative proposal to clarify the terms and conditions thereof

event_claims.description: After the go-shop deadline, if the board determined an alternative proposal was or could reasonably be expected to result in a superior proposal, the Company could provide information and access under compliant confidentiality agreements.
event_claims.quote_text: if the board determines in good faith that the alternative proposal constitutes or could reasonably be expected to result in a superior proposal

event_claims.description: Since the go-shop deadline, the Company was required to notify Hudson’s Bay of entry into a qualifying confidentiality agreement or receipt of an alternative proposal or related proposal or offer.
event_claims.quote_text: Since 12:01 a.m., New York time, on September 6, 2013, the Company has been and is required to promptly (and in any event within 48 hours) notify Hudson’s Bay of its entry into a confidentiality agreement meeting certain requirements or its receipt of an alternative proposal or amendment thereto

event_claims.description: Before shareholder approval, if the Company received an alternative proposal that the board determined constituted a superior proposal, the board could terminate the merger agreement to enter into an acquisition agreement for that superior proposal, subject to merger agreement obligations and the termination fee.
event_claims.quote_text: the board may, subject to compliance with obligations set forth in the merger agreement, including providing Hudson’s Bay with prior notice and allowing Hudson’s Bay the right to negotiate with the Company to match the terms of any superior proposal, terminate the merger agreement to enter into an acquisition agreement with respect to such superior proposal, subject to payment of the Company termination fee

event_claims.description: The Company could terminate the merger agreement before shareholder approval if the board authorized entry into an alternative acquisition agreement with respect to a superior proposal and related fee and entry conditions were satisfied.
event_claims.quote_text: at any time prior to the approval of the merger agreement by the Company’s shareholders, if (1) the board has authorized the Company to enter into an alternative acquisition agreement with respect to a superior proposal

event_claims.description: The Company would pay Hudson’s Bay a termination fee if it terminated the merger agreement to enter into an alternative acquisition agreement related to a superior proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: the Company terminates the merger agreement to enter into an alternative acquisition agreement related to a superior proposal
